commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh And Samuel told him everything, and hid nothing from him. And he said, It is the LORD: let him do what seems to him good. Some of the Fathers think that these words proceeded from an habitual indifference, as he was not disposed to molest his sons any farther, let the consequences be what they might. Gregory; St. Ephrem) But others believe, that Heli was actuated by the spirit of humanity and resignation, and saved his soul. (Calmet) See chap. The author of the Concord. between the Books of Kings and of Chronicles, thinks Samuel was then 39.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
